title: pachylophus eximius
intro: Pachylophus eximius, a plant commonly known as the "tree marigold", is a flowering shrub native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been linked to its ability to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Pachylophus eximius include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
